conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that. Now let me ask you some questions to assess your eligibility.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 8 years old. I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me ask you some questions about your situation with Alex.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Birmingham.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. Now, does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just one final question to ensure I have all the information I need.

Is Alex currently in hospital or care for an extended period, or are there any other special circumstances regarding his living situation that I should know about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Alex is happy and healthy, just living at home with me and going to primary school as normal.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me review the criteria:

- You live in the UK (Birmingham)
- Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you at home
- You are responsible for him
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital or care
- He's attending primary school normally

Based on the official UK government guidance, you meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

You meet all the requirements:
- You live in the UK
- Alex is under 16 (he's 8 years old)
- You're responsible for him and he lives with you
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can now proceed to apply for Child Benefit. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Birmingham), attends primary school, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. According to UK Child Benefit rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. A person is usually responsible for a child if they live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 8-year-old son Alex, who attends primary school. The claimant is responsible for Alex, they live together, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. There are no special circumstances such as hospital stays or care arrangements. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Birmingham), attends primary school, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant is responsible for Alex as they live together. According to UK Child Benefit rules, a person normally qualifies for Child Benefit if they're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. A person is usually responsible for a child if they live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 8-year-old son Alex, who attends primary school. The claimant is responsible for Alex, they live together, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. There are no special circumstances such as hospital stays or care arrangements. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.
